status: Three lab-confirmed cases: one confirmed death on board (2 May); two confirmed survivors medically evacuated. Two on-board deaths total attributed by ship flag state (one confirmed; one probable).
Two lab-confirmed cases (NICD): one death in a Johannesburg clinic (26 Apr); one survivor in Johannesburg ICU.
Both residents under monitoring at the National Centre for Infectious Diseases tested PCR-negative for hantavirus (8 May 2026); remain in 30-day quarantine per CDA protocol.
Three Canadians self-isolating (two in Ontario, one in Quebec); four Canadians were on board MV Hondius (none symptomatic, PHAC 8 May); ship arrived Tenerife 10 May for evacuation.
Contacts under monitoring; UKHSA confirms 2 British nationals with confirmed hantavirus (hospitalized in ZA and NL); repatriation to Arrowe Park Hospital (Wirral) on 10 May via FCDO charter.

7 US states monitoring residents; 17 Americans to be evaluated at Nebraska Medicine National Quarantine Unit (no mandatory quarantine per CDC, 9 May); 2 NJ residents monitoring after flight contact (not ship passengers); CDC EOC Level 3.
Ship anchored 3-6 May; no community transmission
British national on Tristan da Cunha with suspected hantavirus; exposed during MV Hondius call at the island 13–15 April (UKHSA, 8 May).

Two suspected cases: woman hospitalised in Alicante after sharing a flight with a Hondius passenger who died in Johannesburg (Spain MoH, 8 May); second woman in Catalonia on the same KLM flight, initially missed in contact tracing after switching seats (Spain MoH, 9 May).
German national died on board MV Hondius 2 May 2026 (confirmed Andes virus per WHO DON 600); death attributed to ship flag state (Netherlands) in WHO accounting.
